11. **Info panel** - Bespoke HTML/CSS

For Three.js, I know it's the standard 3D library with a UMD build available on jsDelivr. OrbitControls is the main addon I'll need for camera interaction, though it's typically ESM-only. I should verify the CDN URLs and check if there's a UMD-compatible version or community wrapper available.

For the planet textures, I can pull from Three.js's texture examples which are CORS-enabled. I'll need to verify the exact URLs for Earth, Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune. If individual textures aren't available for all planets, I can generate them procedurally. The procedural approach is actually cleaner and more reliable than hunting for external textures that might not exist for every planet. I'll use Three.js with custom shaders to generate banding for Jupiter and Saturn, craters for rocky planets, and ice patterns for the gas giants, then layer in procedural starfields, orbital paths, and Saturn's rings.

Given the complexity of OrbitControls (~1000 lines), I'll note this as a known constraint. For the solar system card, I can implement a simpler custom camera control or use a UMD wrapper. Let me check if there's a UMD wrapper available.

text:
The user wants a 3D solar system animation using Three.js. I've already:
1. Researched dependencies - Three.js UMD verified, OrbitControls ESM-only
2. Loaded card-class-handbook

Now I need to write the spec. Let me create a comprehensive spec for this solar system card class.

Key decisions:
- Three.js UMD at 0.160.0 (verified 200, UMD format)
- OrbitControls via dynamic import (ESM-only, CARD_SHIM supports top-level await)
- All rendering is Tier 1 (card.js + browser APIs)
- No sidecar needed
- Procedural textures (no external texture assets needed)
- Starfield via THREE.Points (bespoke)
- Orbital mechanics via bespoke math
- Planet labels via HTML overlay
- Lighting via Three.js built-in

Let me write the spec with proper frontmatter.

input.content:
---
card-class:
  name: solar-system
  badge: SST
  defaultTitle: Solar System
  handler: ~
  sidecar: ~
  dependencies:
    umd_scripts:
      - url: "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/three@0.160.0/build/three.min.js"
        format: UMD
        version: "0.160.0"
    styles: []
  subtasks:
    - {name: "3D scene with WebGL renderer", tier: 1, mechanism: "card.js + Three.js UMD", verify: "render_capture"}
    - {name: "camera controls (zoom/pan/rotate)", tier: 1, mechanism: "card.js + OrbitControls ESM import", verify: "render_capture"}
    - {name: "Sun glow and point light", tier: 1, mechanism: "card.js + Three.js built-in lighting", verify: "render_capture"}
    - {name: "8 planets with procedural textures", tier: 1, mechanism: "card.js + procedural canvas textures", verify: "render_capture"}
    - {name: "Saturn rings", tier: 1, mechanism: "card.js + RingGeometry procedural texture", verify: "render_capture"}
    - {name: "starfield background", tier: 1, mechanism: "card.js + THREE.Points", verify: "render_capture"}
    - {name: "orbital path lines", tier: 1, mechanism: "card.js + THREE.Line", verify: "render_capture"}
    - {name: "planet labels (HTML overlay)", tier: 1, mechanism: "card.js + CSS2D-style HTML overlay", verify: "render_capture"}
    - {name: "smooth animation loop", tier: 1, mechanism: "card.js + requestAnimationFrame", verify: "render_capture"}
    - {name: "info panel", tier: 1, mechanism: "card.js + DOM overlay", verify: "render_capture"}
  out_of_scope:
    - "realistic scale distances (compressed for visibility)"
    - "realistic planet size ratios (compressed for visibility)"
    - "asteroid belt"
    - "planetary moons (except Saturn's rings)"
---

# Solar System

## Overview

A canvas card that renders an interactive 3D solar system visualization using Three.js. All 8 planets orbit the Sun at relative speeds following Kepler's laws, with procedural textures that match real planet appearances (gas giant bands, rocky surfaces, ice caps). Saturn has iconic rings. A starfield fills the background. Users can zoom, pan, and rotate the view with mouse controls. Planet names float above each world, and a side panel shows planet info.

## Architecture

All computation happens in the browser — no server-side code needed. Three.js handles the 3D rendering; all planet textures are procedurally generated on `<canvas>` elements at runtime (no external texture assets). Orbital animation uses `requestAnimationFrame` with elapsed time to compute positions.

### Procedural Textures

Each planet gets a texture generated from a 2D canvas element:
- **Mercury**: Gray with darker crater spots
- **Venus**: Yellowish-white with subtle cloud bands
- **Earth**: Blue oceans, green/brown landmasses, white polar caps, subtle cloud layer
- **Mars**: Reddish-brown with darker regions and polar ice cap
- **Jupiter**: Banded stripes in tan, orange, brown, white; Great Red Spot
- **Saturn**: Pale gold bands, similar to Jupiter but more muted
- **Uranus**: Pale cyan/blue-green with subtle banding
- **Neptune**: Deep blue with subtle banding and lighter storm features

All textures are generated once at init, wrapped in `CanvasTexture`, and disposed on cleanup.

### Orbital Mechanics

- Orbital periods follow Kepler's third law: `T² ∝ a³` where `a` is semi-major axis
- Angular speed: `ω = 2π / T` where T is the orbital period in Earth years
- Each planet has a slightly randomized initial orbital phase so they don't all start aligned
- Orbital speeds are relative to Earth = 1 year period at 10 distance units

### Axial Tilts

Each planet is rotated on its axis with its real axial tilt:
- Mercury: ~0.03°
- Venus: ~177.4° (retrograde)
- Earth: ~23.4°
- Mars: ~25.2°
- Jupiter: ~3.1°
- Saturn: ~26.7°
- Uranus: ~97.8°
- Neptune: ~28.3°

### Visual Design

- Dark space background with hundreds of white stars
- Sun at center with emissive glow (yellow/orange with bloom-like effect)
- Planets lit by the Sun's point light (one side bright, one side dark)
- Faint white orbital path lines for each planet
- Planet name labels floating above each planet, always facing the camera
- Info panel on the right side with planet names, relative sizes, and quick facts

## Verified Dependencies

| Dependency | Type | URL | Format | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Three.js 0.160.0 | library | `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/three@0.160.0/build/three.min.js` | UMD | ✅ 200, verified |
| OrbitControls | addon | `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/three@0.160.0/examples/jsm/controls/OrbitControls.js` | ESM | ✅ 200, verified (dynamic import) |
